name: Mulgrew Miller
abstract: Mulgrew Miller (August 13, 1955 – May 29, 2013) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and educator. As a child he played in churches and was influenced on piano by Ramsey Lewis and then Oscar Peterson.

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: A great friend a mine, a pianist... Mulgrew Miller says, “The music is supposed to be singing and dancing all the time.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: The rhythm section swings between Cyrus Chestnut or Mulgrew Miller... I was really impressed with his performances when he worked with the Alumni Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I freelance with a lot of different people as a sideman with Mulgrew Miller
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: For a while it was Mulgrew Miller and Peter Washington and Tony Reedus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: How do you prepare for George Shearing coming on the air, as opposed to, say, Mulgrew Miller? ... Either one of those people, if they’ve got a new piece of music... I’ll get my hands on that... I always do that in the car... what I’m going to say about them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Mulgrew Miller and I were talking about Little Milton Campbell... So during that time I was talking to Mulgrew that I didn’t really understand modes... But so yeah, that was from April 1 until about April 24, man. He said, “I’ve got a gig for you.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010
